issue_description: Customer requested suppression of certain transaction fields, citing privacy concerns related to business partners.
Outgoing transfers matched inbound credits from the same remitter within the same business day.
Correspondent bank requested additional documentation due to insufficient transaction narrative clarity.
A correspondent request was denied due to incomplete compliance fields that were later corrected.
An ongoing payment is maintained after a counterparty relationship is terminated in system records.
A refund was processed after a dispute where the customer could not provide supporting delivery evidence.
Account activity increases after negative screening results are returned, without documented remediation steps.
Card transactions showed sudden spikes in online purchases inconsistent with prior patterns.
Customer attempted to reverse transfers after beneficiary confirmation was received by the bank.
A customer requests to receive funds in a different currency than the declared operating currency.
Customer attempted to add a new payee using altered beneficiary details that were later corrected.
A pattern of payments ended when the recipient requested additional fees outside the documented agreement.
Customer requested fees to be netted against principal despite no fee schedule on file.
The customer provided an originator statement that did not correspond to the actual payer bank records.
Transactions with a counterpart in a sanctioned region continued after the customer affirmed they had no business links there.
Cross-border payments included fees and taxes that were inconsistent across otherwise similar invoices.
Outgoing wire details were provided by the customer rather than sourced from contract documentation.
Customer sought to reverse completed transfers citing “routing errors” without evidence.
Customer requested splitting a single invoice into multiple payments across separate days.
A payment referenced a government agency but lacked any invoice or engagement letter for that purpose.
